perturbation.original_text: 12.2 Clinical Studies: In relation to any Clinical Study, Adaptimmune shall have the *** right to publish or publicly disclose any Data or results arising from such Clinical Study including where such publication arises from the submission of data and/or results to the regulatory authorities. Such right to publish shall not include any MD Anderson Records or any public health information protected by HIPAA or where any publication would be in breach of the Consent and/or Authorization. MD Anderson and/or Principal Investigator shall have the right to independently publish or publicly disclose, either in writing or orally, the Data and results of the Clinical Study/ies after the earlier of the (i) first publication (including any multi-site publication) of such Data and/or results; (ii) twelve (12) months after completion of any multi-site study encompassing any Study or if none, six (6) months after completion of Study. MD Anderson shall, at least thirty (30) days ahead of any proposed date for submission, furnish Adaptimmune with a written copy of the proposed publication or public disclosure. Within such thirty (30) day period, Adaptimmune shall review such proposed publication for any Confidential Information of Adaptimmune provided hereunder or patentable Data. Adaptimmune may also comment on such proposed publication and MD Anderson shall consider such comments in good faith during the aforementioned thirty (30) day period. MD Anderson and/or Principal Investigator shall remove Confidential Information of Adaptimmune provided hereunder that has been so identified (other than Data or Study results), provided that Adaptimmune agrees to act in good faith when requiring the deletion of Adaptimmune Confidential Information. In addition Adaptimmune may request delay of publication for a period not to exceed *** (***) days from the date of receipt of request by MD Anderson, to permit Adaptimmune or Adaptimmune Limited or any Joint Research Partner to file patent applications or to otherwise seek to protect any intellectual property rights contained in such publication or disclosure. Upon such request, MD Anderson shall delay such publication until the relevant protection is filed up to a maximum of *** (***) days from date of receipt of request for delay by MD Anderson.
perturbation.changed_text: 12.2 Clinical Studies: In relation to any Clinical Study, MD Anderson shall have the right to publish or publicly disclose any Data or results arising from such Clinical Study, subject to Adaptimmune's final approval. Such right to publish shall not include any MD Anderson Records or any public health information protected by HIPAA or where any publication would be in breach of the Consent and/or Authorization. Adaptimmune and/or Principal Investigator shall have the right to independently publish or publicly disclose, either in writing or orally, the Data and results of the Clinical Study/ies after the earlier of the (i) first publication (including any multi-site publication) of such Data and/or results; (ii) twelve (12) months after completion of any multi-site study encompassing any Study or if none, six (6) months after completion of Study. MD Anderson shall, at least thirty (30) days ahead of any proposed date for submission, furnish Adaptimmune with a written copy of the proposed publication or public disclosure. Within such thirty (30) day period, Adaptimmune shall review such proposed publication for any Confidential Information of Adaptimmune provided hereunder or patentable Data. Adaptimmune may also comment on such proposed publication, but MD Anderson is not required to implement Adaptimmune comments. MD Anderson and/or Principal Investigator will retain the Confidential Information of Adaptimmune provided hereunder that has been so identified, including Data or Study results. In addition Adaptimmune may request delay of publication indefinitely, to permit Adaptimmune or Adaptimmune Limited or any Joint Research Partner to file patent applications or to otherwise seek to protect any intellectual property rights contained in such publication or disclosure. Upon such request, MD Anderson shall delay such publication until Adaptimmune removes the block of publication.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding publication rights. Initially, Adaptimmune had the primary right to publish first, but the modification gives MD Anderson the first right to publish, but conditioned to Adaptimmune approval. This contradicts the later statements that Adaptimmune has a period to review and request delays to protect its IP, which implies MD Anderson is to publish first. The phrase stating that Adaptimmune can request a delay of publication is contradicted by the phrase were MD Anderson 'shall delay such publication until Adaptimmune removes the block of publication'. The phrase saying that MD Anderson 'is not required to implement Adaptimmune comments' is also a point of contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12.2 Clinical Studies: In relation to any Clinical Study, Adaptimmune shall have the right to publish or publicly disclose any Data or results arising from such Clinical Study including where such publication arises from the submission of data and/or results to the regulatory authorities. Such right to publish shall not include any MD Anderson Records or any public health information protected by HIPAA or where any publication would be in breach of the Consent and/or Authorization. MD Anderson and/or Principal Investigator shall have the right to independently publish or publicly disclose, either in writing or orally, the Data and results of the Clinical Study/ies after the earlier of the (i) first publication (including any multi-site publication) of such Data and/or results; (ii) twelve (12) months after completion of any multi-site study encompassing any Study or if none, six (6) months after completion of Study. MD Anderson shall, at least thirty (30) days ahead of any proposed date for submission, furnish Adaptimmune with a written copy of the proposed publication or public disclosure. Within such thirty (30) day period, Adaptimmune shall review such proposed publication for any Confidential Information of Adaptimmune provided hereunder or patentable Data. Adaptimmune may also comment on such proposed publication and MD Anderson shall consider such comments in good faith during the aforementioned thirty (30) day period. MD Anderson and/or Principal Investigator shall remove Confidential Information of Adaptimmune provided hereunder that has been so identified (other than Data or Study results), provided that Adaptimmune agrees to act in good faith when requiring the deletion of Adaptimmune Confidential Information. In addition Adaptimmune may request delay of publication for a period not to exceed *** (***) days from the date of receipt of request by MD Anderson, to permit Adaptimmune or Adaptimmune Limited or any Joint Research Partner to file patent applications or to otherwise seek to protect any intellectual property rights contained in such publication or disclosure. Upon such request, MD Anderson shall delay such publication until the relevant protection is filed up to a maximum of *** (***) days from date of receipt of request for delay by MD Anderson.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a significant in-text contradiction regarding publication rights. Initially, Adaptimmune had the primary right to publish first. The modification gives MD Anderson the first right to publish but conditioned to Adaptimmune approval. This contradicts the later statements that Adaptimmune has a period to review and request delays to protect its IP, which implies MD Anderson is to publish first. The phrase stating that Adaptimmune can request a delay of publication is contradicted by the phrase were MD Anderson 'shall delay such publication until Adaptimmune removes the block of publication'. The phrase saying that MD Anderson 'is not required to implement Adaptimmune comments' is also a point of contradiction. This creates a strong contradiction in Section 12.2, making the publication process and rights unclear and potentially unenforceable. The definition of ambiguities - in text contradiction applies.

perturbation.original_text: 1.9 MD Anderson represents and certifies that neither MD Anderson nor Principal Investigator will, directly or indirectly, offer or pay, or authorize an offer or payment of, any money or anything of value to any Public Official (defined below) or public entity, with the knowledge or intent that the payment, promise or gift, in whole or in part, will be made in order to improperly influence an official act or decision that will assist Adaptimmune in securing an improper advantage or in obtaining or retaining business or in directing business to any person or entity in relation to the Study. In addition to other rights or remedies under this Agreement or at law, Adaptimmune may terminate the affected Study Order if MD Anderson breaches any of the representations or certifications contained in this Section or if Adaptimmune learns that improper payments are being or have been made to any Public Official by MD Anderson or Investigator. For the purposes of this Agreement, "Public Official" means any officer or employee of a government, a public international organization or any department or agency thereof, or any person acting in an official capacity, including, for a public agency or enterprise; and any political party or party official, or any candidate for public office. Adaptimmune acknowledges and agrees that MD Anderson is an agency of the State of Texas, and its investigator, employees, and officers do constitute a Public Official, as used in this paragraph, for purposes of this Section. Notwithstanding anything in this Section 1.9, nothing in this Section shall constitute a limitation on MD Anderson's ability to operate within its legal capacity as an agency of the State of Texas, nor shall anything in this Agreement require MD Anderson to violate any law or to refrain from complying with any law applicable to MD Anderson.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.9 MD Anderson represents and certifies that neither MD Anderson nor Principal Investigator will, directly or indirectly, offer or pay, or authorize an offer or payment of, any money or anything of value to any Public Official (defined below) or public entity, with the knowledge or intent that the payment, promise or gift, in whole or in part, will be made in order to improperly influence an official act or decision that will assist Adaptimmune in securing an improper advantage or in obtaining or retaining business or in directing business to any person or entity in relation to the Study. However, MD Anderson is allowed to make reasonable hospitality payments to Public Officials (defined below). In addition to other rights or remedies under this Agreement or at law, Adaptimmune may terminate the affected Study Order if MD Anderson breaches any of the representations or certifications contained in this Section or if Adaptimmune learns that improper payments are being or have been made to any Public Official by MD Anderson or Investigator, except for reasonable hospitality payments. For the purposes of this Agreement, "Public Official" means any officer or employee of a government, a public international organization or any department or agency thereof, or any person acting in an official capacity, including, for a public agency or enterprise; and any political party or party official, or any candidate for public office. Adaptimmune acknowledges and agrees that MD Anderson is an agency of the State of Texas, and its investigator, employees, and officers do constitute a Public Official, as used in this paragraph, for purposes of this Section. Notwithstanding anything in this Section 1.9, nothing in this Section shall constitute a limitation on MD Anderson's ability to operate within its legal capacity as an agency of the State of Texas, nor shall anything in this Agreement require MD Anderson to violate any law or to refrain from complying with any law applicable to MD Anderson.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction in the restrictions against payments to public officials. The original text strictly prohibits any payments intended to improperly influence decisions. The added text permits 'reasonable hospitality payments,' creating ambiguity regarding what constitutes an improper influence versus acceptable hospitality. The initial statement saying Adaptimmune may terminate the affected Study Order due to improper payments by MD Anderson, is contradicted by the phrase stating 'except for reasonable hospitality payments', which creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.9 MD Anderson represents and certifies that neither MD Anderson nor Principal Investigator will, directly or indirectly, offer or pay, or authorize an offer or payment of, any money or anything of value to any Public Official (defined below) or public entity, with the knowledge or intent that the payment, promise or gift, in whole or in part, will be made in order to improperly influence an official act or decision that will assist Adaptimmune in securing an improper advantage or in obtaining or retaining business or in directing business to any person or entity in relation to the Study. In addition to other rights or remedies under this Agreement or at law, Adaptimmune may terminate the affected Study Order if MD Anderson breaches any of the representations or certifications contained in this Section or if Adaptimmune learns that improper payments are being or have been made to any Public Official by MD Anderson or Investigator. For the purposes of this Agreement, "Public Official" means any officer or employee of a government, a public international organization or any department or agency thereof, or any person acting in an official capacity, including, for a public agency or enterprise; and any political party or party official, or any candidate for public office. Adaptimmune acknowledges and agrees that MD Anderson is an agency of the State of Texas, and its investigator, employees, and officers do constitute a Public Official, as used in this paragraph, for purposes of this Section. Notwithstanding anything in this Section 1.9, nothing in this Section shall constitute a limitation on MD Anderson's ability to operate within its legal capacity as an agency of the State of Texas, nor shall anything in this Agreement require MD Anderson to violate any law or to refrain from complying with any law applicable to MD Anderson.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception ('reasonable hospitality payments') to the original blanket prohibition on payments to public officials. This creates a direct contradiction. The original text explicitly states that Adaptimmune may terminate the Study Order if MD Anderson makes improper payments. The changed text then allows for 'reasonable hospitality payments,' creating a direct contradiction and ambiguity regarding which payments are permissible and which would trigger termination, creating a direct contradiction within section 1.9

perturbation.original_text: 2.3 MD Anderson shall and will ensure that each Principal Investigator shall conduct a Study in accordance with (a) the terms and conditions of this Agreement and the relevant Study Order, (b) the provisions of the Protocol or workscope, as applicable, (c) applicable Good Clinical Practice requirements as incorporated by FDA regulations ("GCP"), (d) the ethical principles of the Declaration of Helsinki, as applicable, and (e) any and all applicable orders and mandates of relevant authorities (including the FDA) and IRB, and applicable MD Anderson policies. MD Anderson shall ensure that all persons participating in any Study are either employees of MD Anderson or are under legally binding obligations to MD Anderson requiring performance in accordance with the terms of this Agreement and that all persons 4 conducting any Study are properly trained with respect to their tasks performed for the Study. The Study shall be conducted at MD Anderson. Only Adaptimmune shall be entitled to amend or modify the Protocol, which amendments and modification must be approved by the IRB prior to implementation. Neither MD Anderson or Principal Investigator shall be entitled to amend any Protocol for any Study except as necessary to eliminate any immediate hazard to the safety, rights or welfare of the Study patient or unless required by the IRB. Any deviation from the Protocol must be agreed by Adaptimmune in advance unless necessary to eliminate an apparent immediate hazard to the safety, rights or welfare of any Study patient or unless required by the IRB. MD Anderson will promptly report any known deviation to Adaptimmune.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.3 MD Anderson shall and will ensure that each Principal Investigator shall conduct a Study in accordance with (a) the terms and conditions of this Agreement and the relevant Study Order, (b) the provisions of the Protocol or workscope, as applicable, (c) applicable Good Clinical Practice requirements as incorporated by FDA regulations ("GCP"), (d) the ethical principles of the Declaration of Helsinki, as applicable, and (e) any and all applicable orders and mandates of relevant authorities (including the FDA) and IRB, and applicable MD Anderson policies. MD Anderson shall ensure that all persons participating in any Study are either employees of MD Anderson or are under legally binding obligations to MD Anderson requiring performance in accordance with the terms of this Agreement and that all persons 4 conducting any Study are properly trained with respect to their tasks performed for the Study. The Study shall be conducted at MD Anderson. Either Adaptimmune or MD Anderson may amend or modify the Protocol, which amendments and modification must be approved by the IRB prior to implementation. MD Anderson or Principal Investigator shall be entitled to amend any Protocol for any Study for any reason, with notification to Adaptimmune within 30 days after amendment. Any deviation from the Protocol need not be agreed by Adaptimmune in advance, but must be communicated to Adaptimmune within 30 days following the deviation. MD Anderson will promptly report any known deviation to Adaptimmune.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates conflicting statements regarding who can amend the study protocol and when. Initially, only Adaptimmune could amend the protocol, and deviations required advance agreement. The modification allows either party to amend the protocol and allows the MD Anderson Principal Investigator to amend it for any reason. Also in this case, creates contradiction stating that any deviation from the protocol need not be agreed by Adaptimmune in advance is contradicting the initial statement where MD Anderson 'shall conduct a Study in accordance with (a) the terms and conditions of this Agreement and the relevant Study Order'. If, MD Anderson Principal Investigator makes any amendments, those can be considered as not being in 'accordance' with initial 'terms and conditions'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.3 MD Anderson shall and will ensure that each Principal Investigator shall conduct a Study in accordance with (a) the terms and conditions of this Agreement and the relevant Study Order, (b) the provisions of the Protocol or workscope, as applicable, (c) applicable Good Clinical Practice requirements as incorporated by FDA regulations ("GCP"), (d) the ethical principles of the Declaration of Helsinki, as applicable, and (e) any and all applicable orders and mandates of relevant authorities (including the FDA) and IRB, and applicable MD Anderson policies. MD Anderson shall ensure that all persons participating in any Study are either employees of MD Anderson or are under legally binding obligations to MD Anderson requiring performance in accordance with the terms of this Agreement and that all persons 4 conducting any Study are properly trained with respect to their tasks performed for the Study. The Study shall be conducted at MD Anderson. Only Adaptimmune shall be entitled to amend or modify the Protocol, which amendments and modification must be approved by the IRB prior to implementation. Neither MD Anderson or Principal Investigator shall be entitled to amend any Protocol for any Study except as necessary to eliminate any immediate hazard to the safety, rights or welfare of the Study patient or unless required by the IRB. Any deviation from the Protocol must be agreed by Adaptimmune in advance unless necessary to eliminate an apparent immediate hazard to the safety, rights or welfare of any Study patient or unless required by the IRB. MD Anderson will promptly report any known deviation to Adaptimmune.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a significant in-text contradiction. The original text states, 'Only Adaptimmune shall be entitled to amend or modify the Protocol,' while the changed text states, 'Either Adaptimmune or MD Anderson may amend or modify the Protocol...' This directly contradicts the previous exclusivity granted to Adaptimmune. Additionally, the changed text allows MD Anderson to amend the Protocol 'for any reason,' with only a 30-day notification, which contradicts the original requirement that 'Any deviation from the Protocol must be agreed by Adaptimmune in advance.' This clear and direct conflict would cause confusion and potential unenforceability, as the document provides conflicting instructions regarding protocol amendments.
